title: grindelia stricta var. macrophylla
intro: Grindelia stricta var. macrophylla, also known as gumweed or broadleaf gumweed, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Grindelia stricta var. macrophylla include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and soothing coughs, while also supporting immune function and promoting overall well-being. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in herbal remedies to treat a range of health conditions.
